mission.title: Install and Configure Vitess on Kubernetes
mission.description: Vitess is a cloud-native horizontally-scalable distributed database system built around MySQL, enabling unlimited scaling through generalized sharding. You would install it to manage large-scale databases efficiently and maintain performance as your application grows.

steps.title: Create a namespace for Vitess
steps.description:
First, create a dedicated namespace for Vitess to operate within your Kubernetes cluster. This helps in organizing resources and managing access.
```bash
kubectl create namespace vitess
```

steps.title: Deploy Vitess components
steps.description:
Apply the Vitess manifests to deploy the necessary components. Make sure to use the correct version.
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vitessio/vitess/v23.0.3/deploy/kubernetes/vitess.yaml --namespace vitess
```

steps.title: Verify the deployment
steps.description:
Check that the Vitess pods are running correctly. This step ensures that all components have been deployed successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace vitess
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the Vitess components to ensure they operate within defined resource constraints. Modify the deployment YAML as needed and apply it.
```bash
kubectl edit deployment vitess --namespace vitess
# Add resource limits under spec.template.spec.containers.resources
```

steps.title: Expose Vitess services
steps.description:
To access Vitess from outside the cluster, expose the Vitess services using a LoadBalancer or NodePort service type.
```bash
kubectl expose deployment vitess --type=LoadBalancer --name=vitess-service --namespace vitess
```

uninstall.title: Delete the Vitess deployment
uninstall.description:
Remove the Vitess deployment from your Kubernetes cluster to clean up resources.
```bash
kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vitessio/vitess/v23.0.3/deploy/kubernetes/vitess.yaml --namespace vitess
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Vitess namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the namespace created for Vitess to ensure all resources are cleaned up.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace vitess
```

upgrade.title: Backup current Vitess state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your current Vitess data and configuration.
```bash
# Perform backup steps as per your data management policy
```

upgrade.title: Update the Vitess manifests
upgrade.description:
Fetch the latest Vitess manifests to prepare for the upgrade.
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vitessio/vitess/v23.0.3/deploy/kubernetes/vitess.yaml --namespace vitess
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the Vitess pods to ensure they are running the new version correctly.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace vitess
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If your Vitess pods are stuck in a Pending state, check for resource availability in the cluster.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace vitess
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If the Vitess service is not reachable, ensure that the service is correctly exposed and check the service type.
```bash
kubectl get services --namespace vitess
```

troubleshooting.title: High memory usage
troubleshooting.description:
If you notice high memory usage, consider adjusting the resource limits set for the Vitess components.
```bash
kubectl top pods --namespace vitess
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Vitess will have all pods running in the 'Running' state and the services accessible as configured. You should also be able to interact with the Vitess database as intended.
codeSnippets:
kubectl edit deployment vitess --namespace vitess
# Add resource limits under spec.template.spec.containers.resources

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher and kubectl installed to manage your cluster.
